blocks:
Ayahs

وَٱلضُّحَىٰ (۱)
By the morning brightness, (1)
وَٱلَّيْلِ إِذَا سَجَىٰ (۲)
and by the night when it is calm! (2)
مَا وَدَّعَكَ رَبُّكَ وَمَا قَلَىٰ (۳)
Your Lord has neither forsaken you, nor is He displeased with you, (3)
وَلَلْـَٔاخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لَّكَ مِنَ ٱلْأُولَىٰ (٤)
and the Hereafter shall be better for you than the world. (4)
وَلَسَوْفَ يُعْطِيكَ رَبُّكَ فَتَرْضَىٰٓ (٥)
Soon your Lord will give you [that with which] you will be pleased. (5)

Key Points

Ḍuḥā (ضحى) means the early part of the day and the time of forenoon. Sajā (سَجى) means both covering and stillness and tranquility. Wadda‘aka (وَدّعك), derived from tawdi‘ (تودیع), means abandoning and forsaking. And qalā (قلى) means intense enmity, hatred, anger, and wrath. For a few days, revelation was cut off from the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. The opponents criticized Him, saying that God had forsaken Him. These verses were revealed to console the Prophet. Perhaps the reason for the cessation of revelation was for Allah to make people understand that everything originates from Him, and that the Prophet has no control over the words of the revelation, the time of its descent, or its quantity. The sign that Allah has not forsaken His Prophet is that in this small Surah, the Prophet Himself is addressed thirteen times.

Messages

1- The brightness of the day and the darkness and tranquility of the night are two great Divine blessings by which Allah has sworn. «و الضّحى و الّیل اذا سجى»

2- Resolutely silence the tongues of the slanderers and win the hearts of the pure. «والضحى و الّیل اذا سجى ما ودّعك ربّك»

3- Explicitly respond to incorrect judgments, prejudices, and analyses. «ما ودّعك ربُّك»

4- A mentor should not abandon the one under His tutelage. «ما ودّعك ربُّك»

5- Do not consider a temporary cessation of grace as a sign of Divine neglect or wrath. «ما ودّعك ربُّك و ما قلى»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Ad-Duha

This Surah has eleven verses and was revealed in Mecca. The name of the Surah is taken from the first verse, which begins with an oath by the time of the rising day. Since the opponents were slandering the Prophet due to the cessation of the descent of revelation for a period of fifteen days, this Surah serves to console the Prophet and describes Allah's support for Him, from the time of His orphanhood until He reached prophethood. It then asks the Prophet to also treat orphans and the deprived with a Divine demeanor, and to manifest and be grateful for the Divine blessings both through word and deed.

Ayahs

أَلَمْ يَجِدْكَ يَتِيمًا فَـَٔاوَىٰ (٦)
Did He not find you an orphan, and shelter you? (6)
وَوَجَدَكَ ضَآلًّا فَهَدَىٰ (۷)
Did He not find you astray, and guide you? (7)
وَوَجَدَكَ عَآئِلًا فَأَغْنَىٰ (۸)
Did He not find you needy, and enrich you? (8)
فَأَمَّا ٱلْيَتِيمَ فَلَا تَقْهَرْ (۹)
So, as for the orphan, do not oppress him; (9)
وَأَمَّا ٱلسَّآئِلَ فَلَا تَنْهَرْ (۱٠)
and as for the beggar, do not chide him; (10)
وَأَمَّا بِنِعْمَةِ رَبِّكَ فَحَدِّثْ (۱۱)
and as for your Lord’s blessing, proclaim it! (11)

Key Points

The meaning of "the Hereafter" (al-Akhirah) in the verse "و للاخرة خیر لك من الاولى" (And the Hereafter is better for you than the first [life]) is either the Day of Judgment or the ultimate outcome of the Prophet's affairs in this very world.

Before the birth of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, his father Abdullah passed away. In his childhood, he lost his mother, and when he was eight years old, his grandfather Abdul Muttalib died. Thus, He was aware of the suffering of orphans.

The Quran states: "ما ضلّ صاحبكم و ما غوى" (84) meaning your Prophet was not misguided even for a moment. Therefore, the intent of "وجدك ضالاً فهدى" (And found you lost and guided [you]) is that had it not been for Divine guidance, you would not have reached your destination. Just as in Surah Ash-Shura, Ayah 52, He says: "ما كنت تَدرى ما الكتاب و لا الایمان ولكن جعلناه نورا نهدى به" (You did not know what the Scripture was or [what] the faith [was], but We made it a light by which We guide). If Our grace had not been there, you would have known nothing of the Scripture and faith. And in Surah Yusuf, Ayah 3, He also says: "اوحینا الیك هذا القرآن و ان كنت من قبله لمن الغافلین" (We reveal to you this Quran, although before it you were among the unaware). Before the revelation of the Quran, you were among the unaware.

The word ḍāll (ضالّ) means both misguided and lost, similar to "الحكمة ضالة المؤمن" (85) (Wisdom is the lost property of the believer). Yes, the Prophet was perplexed before the descent of revelation, and through the light of revelation, He became acquainted with His Divine commands and duty. ‘Ā’il (عائل) refers to the poor person, and qahr (قهر) is dominance accompanied by humiliation. Nahr (نهر) is driving away accompanied by harshness. The term nahr (river) is also used because of the swift movement of the water.

If the needs of the orphan are not met, numerous moral and social corruptions threaten society, and a lack of affection might turn the orphan into a hard-hearted and complex-ridden individual.

We read in a Hadith: The most hopeful verse in the Quran is the verse "و لسوف یعطیك ربّك فترضى" (And your Lord is going to give you, and you will be satisfied), which refers to intercession (Shafa'ah) (86). The satisfaction of the Prophet, both in this world and the Hereafter, is the focus of Allah's attention. Just as in this world, He changed the Qibla (direction of prayer) for His satisfaction, so that the Jews would not hold it against Him that He was praying towards Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem), which was their Qibla. "فلنولینك قبلةً ترضاها" (We will surely turn you to a Qibla with which you will be pleased). And in the Hereafter, He grants Him the station of intercession so that He may be satisfied. "و لسوف یعطیك ربّك فترضى" (87).

Imam Baqir عليه السلام swore twice that the intent of that special gift which Allah will bestow upon His Prophet is intercession. "واللّه الشفاعة واللّه الشفاعة" (By Allah, intercession! By Allah, intercession!) (88). And Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The satisfaction of my grandfather is that no monotheist remains in the Fire (89).

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, regarding the verse "و امّا بنعمة ربّك فحدّث" (But as for the favor of your Lord, report [it]), said: "حدّث بما اعطاك اللّه و فضّلك و رزقك و احسن الیك و هداك" (90) (Report what Allah has given you, favored you with, provided you with, shown kindness to you, and guided you). The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: "التحدّث بنعمة اللّه شكر و تركه كفر" (91) (Speaking of Allah's favor is gratitude, and abandoning it is disbelief). Whoever's life does not show the effects of Divine blessings is an enemy of Allah and an opponent of His favors.

84) Najm, 2. 85) Nahj al-Balaghah, Hikmah 80. 86) Shawahid al-Tanzil, Vol. 2, p. 446. 87) 88) Bihar, Vol. 8, p. 57. 89) Tafsir Safi. 90) Tafasir Majma' al-Bayan and Namuneh. 91) Wasail, Vol. 8, p. 40.

Messages

  1. Allah has insured His Prophet against all forms of wrath and anger in this world and the Hereafter. "وللاخرة خیر لك..."

  2. The blessings of the Hereafter do not have the transience, suffering, or deficiency of worldly blessings. "وللاخرة خیر لك من الاولى"

  3. In response to the temporary cessation of revelation, Allah gives His Prophet a significant promise. "ولسوف یعطیك ربُّك"

  4. Even the ownership of the entire world does not satisfy the men of God; therefore, the fulfillment of Allah's promise lies in another world. "لسوف یعطیك ربّك فترضى"

  5. Gaining the Prophet's satisfaction is the focus of Allah's attention. "فترضى"

  6. A leader must be acquainted with suffering in order to feel the pain of others. "الم یجدك یتیماً، عائلاً"

  7. Orphanhood is not an obstacle to reaching perfection. "الم یجدك"

  8. It is ugly for one human to confer favor upon another, but Allah's favor upon humanity is a value. "الم یجدك یتیماً فاوى..."

  9. Remembering Divine blessings revives the spirit of gratitude towards Allah and service to others in a person. "الم یجدك یتیماً فاوى... فاما الیتیم فلا تقهر"

  10. When you are powerful, remember your time of weakness. "الم یجدك یتیماً"

  11. Divine favors are mediated. "آوى" (Allah provided shelter and enriched [the Prophet] through Hadrat Abu Talib and Hadrat Khadijah).

  12. Giving shelter to the orphan and enriching the poor is a Divine act. "فاوى - فاغنى"

  13. If you remember your bitter past, you will not forbid or reject the deprived. "فاما الیتیم فلا تقهر"

  14. Attending to the orphan is a priority due to his age and psychological condition. "فلا تقهر - فلا تنهر"

  15. Know that blessings come from Allah and do not be arrogant about them. "نعمة ربّك"

  16. Reporting a blessing is a form of gratitude. "فحدّث"

  17. Allah's past grace is a factor for hope in the future. (The God Who removed your orphanhood, poverty, and perplexity in the past will surely make your future and Hereafter better.) "وللاخرة خیر لك... الم یجدك..."

  18. Allah gives much but asks for little. He enriches the poor, but He does not ask us to enrich the poor; He only says: Do not drive away the poor. "و امّا السائل فلا تنهر" He gives shelter to the orphan, but He only asks us not to dominate the orphan. "و امّا الیتیم فلا تقهر"

  19. Allah gives without being asked, so we should at least help others after they ask and request. "ووجدك عائلا فاغنى... و امّا السائل فلا تنهر"

  20. Do not consider blessings the product of your own knowledge, expertise, or cleverness, but rather know them to be His grace. "بنعمة ربّك..."

  21. Remembering blessings saves a person from despair and self-defeat and increases his resilience against calamities. "بنعمة ربّك فحدّث"

  22. More important than food and clothing for orphans is affection. "امّا الیتیم فلا تقهر"

  23. If illness, poverty, or hardship befalls a person, remembering other blessings is a balm for him. "و امّا بنعمة ربّك فحدّث"

  24. Divine blessings must be used for the growth and education of man, not for his heedlessness. "نعمة ربّك"
